chunk_summary: This section focuses on inpatient stabilization feeding and fluid safety protocols for children with severe acute malnutrition, emphasizing macronutrient needs and clinical guidelines for safe management.
learning_objectives: Understand the principles of inpatient stabilization feeding for severe acute malnutrition (SAM).
Learn fluid management protocols to prevent complications in children with SAM.
Identify key macronutrient requirements during the stabilization phase.
Review evidence-based guidelines for managing SAM in clinical settings.
key_facts: Children with severe acute malnutrition require therapeutic feeding with energy-dense formulas such as F-75 during the stabilization phase.
Fluid management must be cautious to avoid overhydration; intravenous fluids are only recommended in cases of shock or severe dehydration.
WHO guidelines recommend a daily energy intake of 100 kcal/kg/day during the stabilization phase.
Micronutrient supplementation, including vitamin A, zinc, and potassium, is critical to address deficiencies.
Rehydration solutions for malnutrition (ReSoMal) are preferred over standard oral rehydration solutions (ORS) for children with SAM.
Close monitoring of vital signs and anthropometric measurements is essential to track progress and prevent complications.
reference_quote: WHO guidelines recommend using F-75 therapeutic milk during the stabilization phase to provide 100 kcal/kg/day energy intake while avoiding overhydration risks.
document_key_topics: Fluid management and safety in SAM
